title: Churn Prediction
tagline: Predict which customers churn in 90 days from usage and profile data

sections.problem: The business needed to identify customers at risk of churning in the next 90 days so retention campaigns could be targeted. A generic rule (e.g. "no login in 30 days") had high false positives and wasted effort.
sections.data: I used a synthetic dataset of 10k customers with profile (tenure, plan, region) and behavior (logins, support tickets, usage). Churn rate was ~15%, so the dataset was imbalanced. I split 80/20 with stratification and held out a temporal slice for validation.
sections.approach: Baseline: logistic regression on a few raw features (F1 ≈ 0.32). I added engineered features (sessions per week, ticket trend, tenure bucket), tried class weighting and SMOTE, and moved to XGBoost with tuned hyperparameters. Evaluation was F1 and PR-AUC to account for imbalance.
sections.results: XGBoost with engineered features and class weighting reached F1 0.50 and PR-AUC 0.58. Feature importance pointed to recent activity and support volume as the strongest signals. The ROC and PR curves below compare baseline vs final model.
next: Deploy as a batch job and feed scores into the CRM.
Run a retention A/B test to measure impact on actual churn.
Add SHAP explanations for top-risk users.
